{'answer': 'Based on my research of current Polymarket trading bot strategies and bet sizing methodologies, I\'ll provide a comprehensive graduated bet_max ladder design for your untested 0.80-0.88 entry zone strategy. Let me combine insights from successful 2026 trading approaches with established risk management principles.\n\n## Graduated Bet_Max Tier System\n\n### **Tier Structure**\n\n| Tier | Unlock Criteria | Bet_Max | Notes |\n|------|-----------------|---------|--------|\n| **T1 (Starting)** | 0 trades, assumed 65% WR | $15 | Current cap |\n| **T2 (Rookie)** | 25 trades, 68% WR, $500+ bankroll | $25 | Conservative scale |\n| **T3 (Proven)** | 75 trades, 70% WR, $1,500+ bankroll | $40 | Statistical confidence |\n| **T4 (Veteran)** | 150 trades, 72% WR, $3,000+ bankroll | $65 | Kelly-informed |\n| **T5 (Elite)** | 300 trades, 74% WR, $6,000+ bankroll | $100 | Maximum cap |\n\n### **Drawdown Circuit-Breaker Rules**\n\n\nBased on prop firm standards of 5% daily and 10% total drawdown limits\n, here\'s your tier-dropping system:\n\n- **Tier Drop Trigger**: Rolling 30-day drawdown exceeds 8% of current bankroll\n- **Emergency Stop**: Single-day loss exceeds 4% of bankroll → immediate drop to T1\n- **Recovery Gate**: Must achieve 15 consecutive days with net-positive P&L to regain previous tier\n\n## Kelly/Variance Analysis\n\n### **Theoretical Win Rate Safety Zones**\n\n\nFor most trading strategies, Kelly suggests 2-4% risk per trade, with recommendations to use 25-50% of Kelly for more stable returns\n.\n\nFor your 14% gross edge scenario:\n- **Break-even threshold**: 63-68% (as you noted)\n- **T2 unlock at 68% WR**: ~5% Kelly position sizing becomes safe\n- **T4 at 72% WR**: ~8% Kelly positioning justified\n- **T5 at 74% WR**: Full 10%+ Kelly acceptable\n\n\nThe mathematics of fractional Kelly reveal that betting slightly less than Kelly costs little in expected growth but dramatically reduces variance and drawdown risk\n.\n\n## Win Rate Assumption for T1\n\n**Recommended T1 assumption: 65% WR**\n\n**Reasoning**:\n- Your break-even range is 63-68%\n- \nAI consensus models (GPT-4, Claude, fine-tuned models) showing 14-point edges are tradable\n\n- Conservative buffer above break-even but below required T2 threshold\n- \nWith fewer than 50-100 trades, estimates are noisy and Kelly percentage will be inaccurate, potentially leading to dangerous over-sizing\n\n\n## Implementation Traps & Gotchas\n\n### **1. Sample Size Trap**\n\nDrawing strategic conclusions from smaller samples on Polymarket is almost always misleading due to variance\n. Your T2 requirement of 25 trades is minimal - consider requiring 50+ before any increases.\n\n### **2. Market Evolution Risk**  \n\nThe traders who win in 2027 won\'t be the ones with the best 2026 strategies. They\'ll be the ones with adaptive systems that evolve with the market\n. Your 0.80-0.88 zone is untested and may face efficiency pressure.\n\n### **3. Correlation Blindness**\nYour 2-rung ladder creates position correlation risk. \nBots must account for time decay, information asymmetry, liquidity depth, and sudden repricing when new data arrives\n. Consider reducing bet_max by 25% when both ladder rungs are active.\n\n### **4. Drawdown Calculation Method**\n\nIntraday trailing drawdown recalculates the floor tick-by-tick based on highest unrealized equity. The floor moves up the moment any open position moves into profit. It does not move back down. This is what kills accounts on winning trades\n. \n\nUse **end-of-day** drawdown calculations to avoid phantom drawdowns from Polymarket\'s price volatility.\n\n## Additional Recommendations\n\n### **Position Sizing Formula**\n```\neffective_bet_size = min(\n    bet_max,\n    bankroll * 0.06,  # Your current 6%\n    (bankroll * kelly_fraction) * 0.5  # Half-Kelly safety\n)\n```\n\n### **Tier Advancement Speed Control**\n\nThe most common mistake at every phase. Your EA enters a normal drawdown, you panic, you change parameters, and you destroy the statistical edge that got you promoted. Doing nothing during drawdown is the smartest move — and the hardest\n.\n\nRequire a 14-day "cooling off" period between tier advancement eligibility and actual advancement to prevent hot-streak gambling.\n\n### **Market Condition Awareness**\n\nPrediction markets evolve as deadlines approach; probabilities often compress toward resolution. Effective automated prediction market trading systems incorporate this dynamic rather than treating prices as static signals\n.\n\nConsider reducing bet_max by 50% for markets closing within 2 hours (your time-decay bonus window).\n\nThis graduated system balances growth potential with capital preservation while accounting for the unique dynamics of prediction markets in 2026.', 'citations': [{'url': 'https://medium.com/@aulegabriel381/the-ultimate-guide-building-a-polymarket-btc-15-minute-trading-bot-with-nautilustrader-ef04eb5edfcb', 'title': 'The Ultimate Guide: Building a Polymarket BTC 15-Minute Trading Bot with NautilusTrader | by Aule Gabriel | Medium', 'snippet': ''}, {'url': 'https://medium.com/@stevenn.hansen/best-polymarket-trading-bots-for-automated-prediction-market-strategies-in-2026-bf06df02823b?postPublishedType=initial', 'title': 'Best Polymarket Trading Bots for Automated Prediction Market Strategies in 2026 | by Steven Hansen | Feb, 2026 | Medium', 'snippet': ''}, {'url': 'https://medium.com/illumination/beyond-simple-arbitrage-4-polymarket-strategies-bots-actually-profit-from-in-2026-ddacc92c5b4f', 'title': 'Beyond Simple Arbitrage: 4 Polymarket Strategies Bots Actually Profit From in 2026 | ILLUMINATION', 'snippet': ''}, {'url': 'https://tradingvps.io/best-polymarket-trading-strategies-in-2026/', 'title': 'Best Polymarket Strategies in 2026 -
